task_completion.description: The agent navigated to the CAMEO Chemicals website.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A web browser window is open, displaying the CAMEO Chemicals homepage or search page (URL: cameochemicals.noaa.gov).

task_completion.description: The agent searched for and viewed the chemical datasheets for Chlorine, Ammonia, and Hydrogen Cyanide.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent viewing the specific chemical datasheets or search results for Chlorine, Ammonia (anhydrous), and Hydrogen Cyanide on the CAMEO website.

task_completion.description: The agent created the required report file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal shows a file named 'aegl_report.txt' being created or edited in the '/home/ga/Documents/' directory.
The text inside 'aegl_report.txt' includes a clear title or header at the top (e.g., 'AEGL Exposure Threshold Report').

task_completion.description: The report contains the 60-minute AEGL-1, AEGL-2, and AEGL-3 values for all three chemicals.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text inside 'aegl_report.txt' lists the 60-minute AEGL-1, AEGL-2, and AEGL-3 values for Chlorine, Ammonia, and Hydrogen Cyanide.

task_completion.description: The report contains a conclusion identifying the most hazardous chemical.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text inside 'aegl_report.txt' includes a concluding sentence or section stating that Chlorine has the lowest AEGL-2 value and/or poses the greatest acute inhalation hazard.

integrity.description: The agent used a graphical web browser to access the CAMEO Chemicals website.
integrity.visual_evidence: A graphical web browser (e.g., Firefox, Chromium) is visible in the screenshots, rather than terminal commands like curl or wget being used to fetch web pages.

integrity.description: The agent used a text editor to compose the report.
integrity.visual_evidence: A text editor (e.g., gedit, nano, vim) is visible being used to write the report, rather than a single terminal command echoing the entire text into the file.

integrity.description: The agent extracted the AEGL values by actually viewing the chemical datasheets.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is seen navigating the CAMEO website to find the AEGL values, rather than hallucinating them or using a pre-existing script.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The correct 60-minute AEGL-2 values are: Chlorine = 2.0 ppm, Ammonia = 160.0 ppm, and Hydrogen Cyanide = 7.1 ppm. The report must conclude that Chlorine has the lowest AEGL-2 value (2.0 ppm) and poses the greatest acute inhalation hazard. The output file must be located at /home/ga/Documents/aegl_report.txt.
